title: The Clifford Number
subtitle: Seven degrees of UK AI state capture, with receipts.
status: AXM Labs experiment, not AXM core.
disclaimer: No wrongdoing is alleged. The graph maps public-role adjacency and sourced institutional relationships. Listed, registered, and attended are separate states.

sources.label: GOV.UK, AI Opportunities Action Plan, 13 Jan 2025
sources.publisher: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology
sources.notes: Official publication of the AI Opportunities Action Plan. Used for recommendations, AI stack framing, government customer framing, public-sector adoption, National Data Library, AI Growth Zones, and UK Sovereign AI.

sources.label: GOV.UK, AI expert to lead Action Plan, 26 Jul 2024
sources.publisher: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology and HM Treasury
sources.notes: Official announcement appointing Matt Clifford to lead the AI Opportunities Action Plan and describing the AI Opportunities Unit.

sources.label: Reuters, Starmer to outline UK AI plan, 12 Jan 2025
sources.notes: Reported that the UK government would implement the 50 recommendations in the AI Opportunities Action Plan.

sources.label: WIRED, Leak Exposes Members of Peter Thiel's Secretive Dialog Society, 16 Jun 2026
sources.notes: Primary public reporting on the Dialog exposure, membership list, 2026 registration list, off-record retreats, and discovery by maia arson crimew.

sources.label: WIRED, Dialog claims it was hacked. A misconfigured website left members exposed, Jun 2026
sources.notes: Public reporting on the misconfiguration theory, exposed registration materials, and authentication failure.

sources.label: The Guardian, Starmer AI adviser to step down, 20 Jun 2025
sources.notes: Used for the reported endpoint of Clifford’s No 10 adviser role and summary that the plan was adopted across government.

sources.label: Local analysis note, Field Autopsy No. 04, Dialog: The Human Layer
sources.notes: Local structural analysis. It is not used as a substitute for public reporting when an official or tier-one source exists.

sources.label: dialog.org HTML source public directory extract, Jun 2026
sources.notes: Public directory names and titles extracted from dialog.org HTML source as recorded in docs/clifford-number-master.md. Listing does not prove attendance or membership.

sources.label: Palantir Investor Relations, Executive Management
sources.notes: Official Palantir executive management page identifying Alexander Karp as co-founder, Chief Executive Officer, and director.

nodes.label: Clifford Policy Machine
nodes.description: Target node. The mapped policy machine around the AI Opportunities Action Plan, the PM adviser role, public-sector adoption, national AI infrastructure, and state-market formation.

nodes.label: Matt Clifford
nodes.description: Co-founder of Entrepreneur First. Commissioned to lead the UK AI Opportunities Action Plan and later appointed as the Prime Minister’s adviser on AI opportunities. Listed in the Dialog public directory extract.

nodes.label: AI Opportunities Action Plan
nodes.description: UK AI policy document published by DSIT on 13 Jan 2025.

nodes.label: Starmer Adoption Layer
nodes.description: The political adoption and implementation layer for the AI Opportunities Action Plan under the UK government.

nodes.label: 10 Downing Street
nodes.description: Prime Minister’s office and political centre of UK government.

nodes.label: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology
nodes.description: UK department responsible for science, innovation, technology, and digital government functions.

nodes.label: HM Treasury
nodes.description: UK government department for finance and economic policy.

nodes.label: AI Opportunities Unit
nodes.description: Unit described by DSIT and HM Treasury as a way to pool expertise and implement proposals from the Action Plan.

nodes.label: Digital Centre of Government
nodes.description: Digital centre in DSIT described in the Action Plan as driving technological transformation of the state.

nodes.label: National Data Library
nodes.description: Action Plan mechanism for identifying and making high-impact public datasets available for AI researchers and innovators.

nodes.label: AI Growth Zones
nodes.description: Action Plan mechanism for accelerated AI data centre buildout and planning approvals.

nodes.label: AI Research Resource
nodes.description: Public compute resource the Action Plan recommended expanding by at least 20x by 2030.

nodes.label: UK Sovereign AI
nodes.description: Action Plan recommendation for a unit that can partner with the private sector to maximise the UK’s stake in frontier AI.

nodes.label: AI Safety Institute
nodes.description: UK AI safety and governance institution referenced as part of the UK’s foundation for AI policy.

nodes.label: Frontier AI Taskforce
nodes.description: Precursor layer to the UK AI Safety Institute.

nodes.label: Entrepreneur First
nodes.description: Talent investor and company builder co-founded by Matt Clifford and Alice Bentinck.

nodes.label: Code First Girls
nodes.description: Coding education organization co-founded by Matt Clifford and Alice Bentinck.

nodes.label: Advanced Research and Invention Agency
nodes.description: UK high-risk research agency. Matt Clifford has been reported as chair.

nodes.label: Dialog
nodes.description: Private invitation-only society co-founded by Peter Thiel and Auren Hoffman, reported to convene public officials, foreign government figures, and Silicon Valley executives at off-record retreats.

nodes.label: Peter Thiel
nodes.description: Co-founder of Founders Fund, Palantir, PayPal, and Dialog.

nodes.label: Auren Hoffman
nodes.description: Dialog co-founder and chairman. Founder of SafeGraph and LiveRamp.

nodes.label: Raffi Grinberg
nodes.description: Executive director of Dialog according to public reporting.

nodes.label: SafeGraph
nodes.description: Location-data company founded by Auren Hoffman.

nodes.label: LiveRamp
nodes.description: Identity-resolution and data onboarding company associated with Auren Hoffman.

nodes.label: Palantir
nodes.description: Data analytics and defense software company co-founded by Peter Thiel and Joe Lonsdale.

nodes.label: Joe Lonsdale
nodes.description: Palantir co-founder and 8VC founder. Listed in the Dialog public directory extract.

nodes.label: Ted Cruz
nodes.description: US senator from Texas. Listed in public Dialog reporting and public directory extracts.

nodes.label: Jim Himes
nodes.description: US representative from Connecticut and ranking member of the House Intelligence Committee. Listed in the Dialog public directory extract.

nodes.label: Dan Driscoll
nodes.description: US Army Secretary in the provided/public Dialog extracts.

nodes.label: Alexus Grynkewich
nodes.description: NATO Supreme Allied Commander Europe and head of US European Command in the provided/public Dialog extracts.

nodes.label: Randy Kroszner
nodes.description: Former Federal Reserve governor and Bank of England Financial Policy Committee member in the provided/public Dialog extracts.

nodes.label: Google DeepMind
nodes.description: Frontier AI company with London headquarters named in the Action Plan.

nodes.label: OpenAI
nodes.description: Frontier AI company named in the Action Plan as having a significant UK office.

nodes.description: Frontier AI company named in the Action Plan as having a significant UK office.

nodes.label: Wayve
nodes.description: UK autonomous vehicle company named in the Action Plan as an emerging local winner.

edges.claim: Matt Clifford is the anchor person for this graph: commissioned to devise the AI Opportunities Action Plan and later tied to the PM adviser implementation layer.
The AI Opportunities Action Plan is the policy object through which the Clifford policy machine is measured.
GOV.UK says Peter Kyle appointed Matt Clifford to deliver the AI Opportunities Action Plan.
DSIT announced Matt Clifford was appointed to kickstart work on the Action Plan.
DSIT and HM Treasury announced the AI Opportunities Unit to pool expertise and implement Action Plan proposals.
The AI Opportunities Unit is described as the implementation expertise pool for Action Plan proposals.
Reuters reported the government would implement the 50 recommendations from Clifford’s AI Opportunities Action Plan.
The Starmer adoption layer is the political implementation face of the Clifford policy machine.
10 Downing Street is the political control node for PM adviser and adoption-layer implementation.
The Action Plan says the Digital Centre of Government will drive technological transformation of the state.
The Action Plan treats the National Data Library as a mechanism for high-impact public datasets for AI.
The Action Plan recommends AI Growth Zones to accelerate AI data centre buildout.
The Action Plan recommends expanding the AI Research Resource by at least 20x by 2030.
The Action Plan recommends creating UK Sovereign AI with power to partner with the private sector.
The Action Plan identifies the AI Safety Institute as part of the UK’s AI governance foundation.
The Frontier AI Taskforce is included as a precursor institution in the Clifford policy timeline.
Matt Clifford co-founded Entrepreneur First.
Matt Clifford is associated with Code First Girls in public profiles.
Matt Clifford has been reported as chair of ARIA.
The provided Dialog directory extract lists Matt Clifford as PM’s Advisor on AI Opportunities, UK Government, and co-founder of Entrepreneur First.
edges.notes: Listed does not equal attended. The graph stores this as a listing edge only.

edges.claim: Dialog is publicly reported as co-founded by Peter Thiel.
Dialog is publicly reported as co-founded by Auren Hoffman, who also served as chairman in the provided extract.
Raffi Grinberg is identified in public reporting and local analysis as Dialog executive director.
Auren Hoffman founded SafeGraph.
Auren Hoffman founded LiveRamp according to the local analysis note.
SafeGraph connects to the graph through founder Auren Hoffman.
LiveRamp connects to the graph through founder Auren Hoffman.
Peter Thiel co-founded Palantir.
Palantir connects to the graph through co-founder Peter Thiel.
Joe Lonsdale co-founded Palantir.
The provided Dialog public-role extract lists Joe Lonsdale in the Dialog layer.
edges.notes: Listed does not equal attended.

edges.notes: Listed does not equal attended.

The Action Plan names Meta AI as having a significant UK office.
